{"society_power": 4.0, "state_power": 3.5, "society_change": 1.0, "state_change": -2.5, "key_event": "Barre's clan-based despotism collapses; armed clan insurgencies rise", "reasoning": "State power fell sharply as Barre hollowed out impersonal institutions into a narrow MOD clan machine reliant on coercion and foreign aid, losing administrative reach amid insurgency and the 1988 Hargeisa bombardment. Society power rose modestly not as civic strength but as armed clan mobilization (SNM, SSDF, USC) reasserted the divisive \"cage of norms,\" pushing Somalia away from the corridor toward stateless conflict."}
